ucs.title: [LLM] BackdoorDiplomacy Turian/Quarian backdoor delivered via signed-binary DLL search-order hijacking
ucs.description: Hunts BackdoorDiplomacy's signature Turian (and predecessor Quarian) backdoor loader pattern, where a legitimate signed binary from a security/AV vendor is dropped into a writable directory alongside a malicious side-loaded DLL, which then beacons over raw TCP on a non-standard port. ESET attributes this exact loader chain to BackdoorDiplomacy targeting MoFA / telco hosts.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_path IN ("*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\PerfLogs\\*","*\\Windows\\Temp\\*","*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*") AND Processes.process_name IN ("vsodscpl.exe","rcs.exe","GoogleUpdate.exe","AppLaunch.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","vmnat.exe","raserver.exe")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest process_id [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name IN ("vsodscpl.dll","mcutil.dll","goopdate.dll","VERSION.dll","libcurl.dll","ssleay32.dll") AND Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\PerfLogs\\*","*\\Windows\\Temp\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_id Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`] | join type=left dest process_id [| tstats summariesonly=true count values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_ports values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as dest_ips from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.app="unknown" OR All_Traffic.dest_port IN (53,443,80,8080,8443,30000,30001,30002,30003) by All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)`] | eval risk_score = case(isnotnull(file_name) AND isnotnull(dest_ports),90, isnotnull(file_name),70, 1=1,40) | where risk_score >= 70 | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | table firstTime lastTime dest user process_name process_path parent_process_name file_name file_path dest_ips dest_ports risk_score
ucs.kql:
let SusPaths = dynamic([@"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\"]);
let AbusedSignedBinaries = dynamic(["vsodscpl.exe","rcs.exe","GoogleUpdate.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","AppLaunch.exe","vmnat.exe","raserver.exe"]);
let HijackedDlls = dynamic(["vsodscpl.dll","mcutil.dll","goopdate.dll","version.dll","libcurl.dll","ssleay32.dll"]);
let LoaderProcs = DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
  | where FileName in~ (AbusedSignedBinaries)
  | where FolderPath has_any (SusPaths)
  | project LoaderTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, LoaderName=FileName, LoaderPath=FolderPath, LoaderPid=ProcessId, LoaderCmd=ProcessCommandLine;
let HijackDllDrops = DeviceFileEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
  | where FileName in~ (HijackedDlls)
  | where FolderPath has_any (SusPaths)
  | project DropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DllName=FileName, DllPath=FolderPath;
let RawTcpBeacons = DeviceNetworkEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
  | where ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess"
  | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (AbusedSignedBinaries)
  | where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (SusPaths)
  | where not(ipv4_is_private(RemoteIP))
  | summarize Beacons=count(), Ports=make_set(RemotePort,10), Dsts=make_set(RemoteIP,10) by DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId, bin(Timestamp,1h);
LoaderProcs
  | join kind=inner (HijackDllDrops) on DeviceId
  | where abs(datetime_diff('minute', LoaderTime, DropTime)) <= 60
  | join kind=leftouter (RawTcpBeacons) on $left.DeviceId == $right.DeviceId, $left.LoaderPid == $right.InitiatingProcessId
  | extend Score = iff(isnotempty(Beacons), 90, 70)
  | project LoaderTime, DeviceName, AccountName, LoaderName, LoaderPath, DllName, DllPath, LoaderCmd, Dsts, Ports, Beacons, Score
  | sort by Score desc, LoaderTime desc
ucs.rationale: Turian/Quarian is BackdoorDiplomacy's hallmark implant. ESET documented that the group consistently abuses signed binaries from Symantec, McAfee, and Google Update by writing them to non-standard directories (ProgramData, PerfLogs, Public) with a same-basename hijack DLL — a combination almost no legitimate software does. Correlating the loader-binary execution + adjacent malicious DLL drop + outb

ucs.title: [LLM] BackdoorDiplomacy China Chopper webshell to EarthWorm/reGeorg tunneler chain on internet-facing IIS/Exchange/F5
ucs.description: Detects BackdoorDiplomacy's documented post-exploitation chain on victim MoFA/telecom edge servers: exploitation of an internet-facing app (F5 BIG-IP CVE-2020-5902, Exchange ProxyLogon, Plesk) leading to a China Chopper .aspx webshell under w3wp/httpd, followed by deployment of the EarthWorm SOCKS tunneler (ew.exe / ew_for_*) or reGeorg to pivot internally.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","nginx.exe","tomcat*.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","quser.exe","arp.exe","ew.exe","ew_for_Win.exe","ew_for_linux.exe","smbexec.exe","reg.exe","schtasks.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process_path Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_id _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval is_recon=if(match(process_name,"(?i)^(whoami|ipconfig|systeminfo|netstat|tasklist|arp|net1?|quser)\.exe$"),1,0), is_tunneler=if(match(process_name,"(?i)^ew(_for_.*)?\.exe$") OR match(process,"(?i)(ssocksd|rcsocks|lcx_slave|reGeorg|tunnel\.aspx)"),1,0), is_shell=if(match(process_name,"(?i)^(cmd|powershell)\.exe$") AND match(parent_process_path,"(?i)(inetpub|exchange|wwwroot|plesk)"),1,0) | stats sum(is_recon) as recon_cmds sum(is_tunneler) as tunneler_cmds sum(is_shell) as shell_cmds values(process_name) as procs values(process) as cmds min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime by dest user parent_process_name parent_process_path | where shell_cmds>=1 AND (tunneler_cmds>=1 OR recon_cmds>=2) | eval risk_score = (shell_cmds*30)+(tunneler_cmds*40)+(recon_cmds*10) | where risk_score >= 50 | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - risk_score
ucs.kql:
let WebHosts = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","nginx.exe","tomcat.exe","java.exe"]);
let ReconBins = dynamic(["whoami.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","netstat.exe","tasklist.exe","arp.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","quser.exe","nbtstat.exe"]);
let TunnelerBins = dynamic(["ew.exe","ew_for_win.exe","ew_for_linux.exe","lcx.exe","htran.exe","frpc.exe"]);
let WebPaths = dynamic([@"\inetpub\", @"\exchange\", @"\wwwroot\", @"\Plesk\", @"\ClientAccess\", @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\"]);
let WebshellChildren = DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
  | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (WebHosts)
  | where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") or FileName in~ (ReconBins) or FileName in~ (TunnelerBins) or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("ssocksd","rcsocks","lcx_slave","reGeorg","tunnel.aspx","-CommandPwd","FromBase64String")
  | extend IsRecon = iff(FileName in~ (ReconBins),1,0),
           IsTunneler = iff(FileName in~ (TunnelerBins) or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("ssocksd","rcsocks","lcx_slave","reGeorg"),1,0),
           IsShell = iff(FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe"),1,0)
  | summarize ReconCnt=sum(IsRecon), TunnelCnt=sum(IsTunneler), ShellCnt=sum(IsShell),
              Cmds=make_set(ProcessCommandLine,25), Procs=make_set(FileName,25),
              FirstSeen=min(Timestamp), LastSeen=max(Timestamp)
    by DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath
  | where ShellCnt >= 1 and (TunnelCnt >= 1 or ReconCnt >= 2);
let WebshellDrops = DeviceFileEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
  | where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx" or FileName endswith ".jsp" or FileName endswith ".php"
  | where FolderPath has_any (WebPaths)
  | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (WebHosts) or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe")
  | summarize WebshellsDropped=make_set(strcat(FolderPath,FileName),10), DropFirst=min(Timestamp) by DeviceId;
WebshellChildren
  | join kind=leftouter (WebshellDrops) on DeviceId
  | extend RiskScore = (ShellCnt*30)+(TunnelCnt*40)+(ReconCnt*10)+iff(isnotempty(WebshellsDropped),20,0)
  | where RiskScore >= 50
  | project FirstSeen, LastSeen, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, ShellCnt, ReconCnt, TunnelCnt, Procs, Cmds, WebshellsDropped, RiskScore
  | sort by RiskScore desc
ucs.rationale: ESET's BackdoorDiplomacy report explicitly names the open-source Chinese-language tools EarthWorm (ew) and reGeorg as the group's preferred SOCKS/HTTP tunnelers, deployed after webshell access on edge servers exploited via T1190 (BIG-IP, Exchange, Plesk). Generic webshell hunts catch many crews; the specific combination of (a) IIS/httpd parent → shell child within the inetpub/Exchange front-end pa
